title: Baleshwar Temple
tagline: Baleshwar Temple — spiritual town of Uttarakhand

overview.short: Baleshwar Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva, situated within city of Champawat in Uttarakhand, India.
overview.description: Baleshwar Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva, situated within city of Champawat in Uttarakhand, India. Built by the rulers of the Chand dynasty, Baleshwar Temple is a marvelous symbol of stone carving. It is believed to have been built in 13th century AD (1390 AD) by Garud Gyan Chand. Uday Chand started reconstruction work for this temple between 1420 and 1421 AD. Hari Chand, who reigned from 1423 AD to 1427 AD, completed the reconstruction work on the temple as per a copperplate inscription (dated 1423 AD) at the temple.

topPlaces.name: Champawat
topPlaces.description: Champawat (Kumaoni: Champāvat) is a town and a Nagar Palika Parishad in Champawat district in the state of Uttarakhand, India. It is the administrative headquarters of Champawat district.

topPlaces.name: Advaita Ashrama
topPlaces.description: Advaita Ashrama, Mayavati, is a branch of the Ramakrishna Math, founded on 19 March 1899 at the behest of Vivekananda, by his disciples James Henry Sevier, and Charlotte Sevier. Today it publishes the original writings of Vivekananda.

topPlaces.name: Lohaghat
topPlaces.description: Lohaghat (Kumaoni: Loghāt) is a town and a nagar palika in Champawat district in the Indian state of Uttarakhand. Lohaghat is also famous for its various cultural events like Holi Rang Mahotsav, Devidhar Mela etc.

topPlaces.name: Khaptad Lake
topPlaces.description: Khaptad Lake (Nepali: खप्तड ताल) is a lake in Doti district of western Nepal. The lake is situated at north-eastern part of Khaptad National Park and an altitude of 3,050 m (10,010 ft).

items.note: Champawat (Kumaoni: Champāvat) is a town and a Nagar Palika Parishad in Champawat district in the state of Uttarakhand, India. It is the administrative headquarters of Champawat district.

Advaita Ashrama, Mayavati, is a branch of the Ramakrishna Math, founded on 19 March 1899 at the behest of Vivekananda, by his disciples James Henry Sevier, and Charlotte Sevier. Today it publishes the original writings of Vivekananda.

Lohaghat (Kumaoni: Loghāt) is a town and a nagar palika in Champawat district in the Indian state of Uttarakhand. Lohaghat is also famous for its various cultural events like Holi Rang Mahotsav, Devidhar Mela etc.
Khaptad Lake (Nepali: खप्तड ताल) is a lake in Doti district of western Nepal. The lake is situated at north-eastern part of Khaptad National Park and an altitude of 3,050 m (10,010 ft).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Baleshwar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Baleshwar Temple is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Baleshwar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pantnagar Airport (69 km) and the nearest railway station is Kathgodam (Nainital railhead) (54 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Baleshwar Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Baleshwar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Baleshwar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Baleshwar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Baleshwar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Champawat, Advaita Ashrama, Lohaghat, Khaptad Lake.
